summary: ISS-1608 (H3 lateral-velocity single reading + dome's angular-velocity language) is closable via patch P1 in EXP-272: substring replacement in data/sections.json part4.html §4.2.7. The current parenthetical contains a math error — it says slower velocity 'leaves the 1.3-km totality-circle diameter unchanged', technically true of the diameter but misleading because per-observer totality DURATION scales as 1.3/v and DOES change (~3 sec at 25 km/min). The patch correctly propagates 0.9 sec @ 87 km/min and ~3 sec @ 25 km/min, adds the 45× shortfall robustness sentence, and provides the angular-velocity cross-check (~2°/min at zenith for rim orbit, ~0.5°/min for mid-disc orbit) so the dome author cannot dismiss the comparison as a unit mismatch.
fix_details_for_close: Prose parenthetical corrected: previously claimed slower velocity left totality-circle diameter unchanged (true but misleading — duration scales as 1.3/v); now correctly propagates ~3 sec at 25 km/min and adds angular-velocity cross-check (~2°/min at zenith for rim orbit, ~0.5°/min for mid-disc orbit) per ISS-1608 unit-system request.
